title: Intensely Umami Japanese Pork Tonkotsu Ramen
description: A rich and savory Japanese ramen dish featuring a deep umami flavor profile, served in an agave leaf vessel. This recipe combines traditional Japanese ingredients with modern cooking techniques to create a truly show-stopping meal.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
Roast the pork bones in the oven for 30 minutes, or until nicely browned.
In a large pot, heat the vegetable oil over medium heat.
Add the sliced onion to the pot and cook, stirring occasionally, until caramelized and golden brown.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger to the pot and cook, stirring constantly, for 1 minute.
Add the bhuna curry powder to the pot and cook, stirring constantly, for 1 minute.
Add the roasted pork bones, chicken broth, and cornstarch to the pot, whisking constantly to avoid lumps.
Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low and simmer, stirring occasionally, for 4 hours.
Cook the ramen noodles according to the package instructions and set aside.
Strain the broth through a fine-mesh sieve into a clean pot, discarding the solids.
Add the cooked noodles, green onion, and sesame oil to the pot and cook, stirring occasionally, for 2 minutes.
